introSection:
Mumbo Jumbo is a steel roller coaster located at Flamingo Land in Malton, North Yorkshire, England, UK. It is an El Loco installation from S&S-Sansei Technologies and was opened on 4 July 2009 on the spot previously occupied by Tidal Wave. The ride's track and supports were designed by Ride Centerline. The total cost of the ride was £4 million.

Upon opening, the ride was the steepest roller coaster with a maximum drop angle of 112°. It was superseded on 2 July 2011 by Timber Drop at Fraispertuis City in France, which was one degree steeper.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Mumbo Jumbo was announced in January 2009.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Mumbo Jumbo has black track and orange supports. The cars share this colour scheme and have flame decals.

sections.title: Name
sections.content: According to park owner Gordon Gibb, the name "is meant to be a tongue-in-cheek description, poking fun at the way other theme parks exaggerate their new rides and attractions. We’re not talking mumbo jumbo - our new rollercoaster is definitely the real deal!".

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: On 3 May 2011, a car got stuck upside-down in the dive loop element after a poncho became lodged between the train wheel and the track. Two riders were trapped for 20 minutes before being rescued.

fullText:
Mumbo Jumbo is a steel roller coaster located at Flamingo Land in Malton, North Yorkshire, England, UK. It is an El Loco installation from S&S-Sansei Technologies and was opened on 4 July 2009 on the spot previously occupied by Tidal Wave. The ride's track and supports were designed by Ride Centerline. The total cost of the ride was £4 million.

Upon opening, the ride was the steepest roller coaster with a maximum drop angle of 112°. It was superseded on 2 July 2011 by Timber Drop at Fraispertuis City in France, which was one degree steeper.

Mumbo Jumbo was announced in January 2009.



Mumbo Jumbo has black track and orange supports. The cars share this colour scheme and have flame decals.



According to park owner Gordon Gibb, the name "is meant to be a tongue-in-cheek description, poking fun at the way other theme parks exaggerate their new rides and attractions. We’re not talking mumbo jumbo - our new rollercoaster is definitely the real deal!".

On 3 May 2011, a car got stuck upside-down in the dive loop element after a poncho became lodged between the train wheel and the track. Two riders were trapped for 20 minutes before being rescued.
